environment.s: Climate-commitment coalition member: RE100 (since 2018; target 2030) and We Mean Business Coalition (since 2018). 100% renewable electricity across all sites by 2030.

privacy.s: Terms-of-service privacy grade D (poor) via ToS;DR (tosdr.org), CC BY-SA 3.0 — transformed to TruNorth's scale.

wiki.extract: Sony Corporation of America is the American arm of Japanese multinational conglomerate Sony Group Corporation. Headquartered in New York City, the company manages Sony's business in the United States.

cases.caseName: Heycock v. Sony Corporation of America
BROADCAST MUSIC INC v. GUSTAVO TWO INC
Allan Joseph Salas v. Rodarius Marcell Green

sampleRecalls.title: Sony Expands Recall of VAIO Laptop Computer Battery Packs Due to Burn and Fire Hazards (Recall Alert)
sampleRecalls.description: This expanded recall involves Panasonic lithium-ion battery packs installed in 18 models of Sony's VAIO Series laptop computers. Sony has expanded the number of affected battery packs under this recall. The Panasonic battery packs were manufactured with the laptop and battery packs were sold separately or installed by…
hazards: The lithium-ion battery packs can overheat, posing burn and fire hazards.
products: Panasonic battery packs used in Sony Electronics laptop computers
units: About 700 (In addition, about 1,700 units were previously recalled on June 15, 2016)

sampleRecalls.title: Sony Recalls VAIO Laptop Computer Battery Packs Due to Burn and Fire Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Panasonic lithium-ion battery packs installed in 18 models of Sony's VAIO Series laptop computers. The Panasonic battery packs were also sold separately and installed by Sony as part of repairs. Panasonic battery packs included in this recall have model number VGP-BPS26 and part numbers 1-853-237-…
hazards: The lithium-ion battery packs can overheat, posing burn and fire hazards.
products: Panasonic battery packs used in Sony Electronics laptop computers
units: About 1,700 (in addition, 21 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: Sony Recalls VAIO Flip PC Laptops Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall includes Sony's VAIO Flip PC laptops with model number (product name) SVF11N13CXS. The computers were sold in three colors silver, black and pink. They have a Panasonic-manufactured lithium-ion battery and a folding touch screen that measures about 11.6 inches diagonally and a backlit keyboard. The VAIO lo…
hazards: The computers' lithium-ion batteries can overheat, posing fire and burn hazards.
injuries: Sony is aware of four incidents, which occurred in Asia, of computers overheating, resulting in units smoking, catching on fire and melting. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: Sony Recalls VAIO Laptop Computers Due to Burn Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled products are VPCF11 Series and VPCCW2 Series notebook computers. The computers are available in many colors and have "VAIO" on the front outside panel. The model numbers can be found on the bottom of the computers. The following models are included: VPCF11 Series VPCF111FD/B VPCF111FX/B VPCF111FX/H VPCF11…
hazards: The computers can overheat, posing a burn hazard to the consumer.
injuries: Sony has received 30 reports of units overheating resulting in deformed keyboards and casings. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: Sony Recalls Computer AC Adapters Due to Shock Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled AC adapter model is the Sony VGP-AC19V17, which was supplied for use with the following Sony products: Product Types Using the AC Adapters Product Models Using the AC Adapters All-in-one VAIO Desktop Computers VGC-LT series and VGC-JS2 series VAIO Docking Stations VGP-PRBX1 and VGP-PRFE1
hazards: Insulation inside the AC adapter can fail over time, posing an electrical shock hazard to consumers.
injuries: Sony has received four reports of adapters short-circuiting. No incidents occurred in the United States. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecords.event_description: Other fall to lower level 6 to 10 feet
sampleRecords.final_narrative: While preparing set props, an employee backed into an unguarded area and fell approximately 10 feet, suffering facial bruises, a right arm radius injury, and a right orbital injury.
